scope: Data-only playable production package; runtime rendering and viewport QA are outside this fixture build.

sourceAndAdaptation.item: Every immutable fact has at least one valid source span.
sourceAndAdaptation.evidence: Strict validation passed; each immutable fact in production.json has a source.main line span.

sourceAndAdaptation.item: Every low-confidence claim that could change the plot remains unresolved or reviewed.
sourceAndAdaptation.evidence: reports/unresolved-claims.json excludes Elias's unproven letter knowledge from canon.

sourceAndAdaptation.item: The adaptation mode and player agency contract are explicit.
sourceAndAdaptation.evidence: production.json and reports/build-request.json declare faithful-stage and witness.

sourceAndAdaptation.item: Every material invention or compression appears in the adaptation ledger.
sourceAndAdaptation.evidence: The sole mechanic translation, staging the open ending as a bounded choice, is recorded.

sourceAndAdaptation.item: No character states knowledge they cannot possess at that beat.
sourceAndAdaptation.evidence: Elias is not assigned knowledge of the accident letter; facts are revealed in source order.

sourceAndAdaptation.item: No UI, optional dialogue, asset label, or objective leaks a gated reveal.
sourceAndAdaptation.evidence: Action labels mention only already-visible actions; required facts reveal at or after earliestReveal.

contentModel.item: IDs and references are stable, unique in scope, and resolvable.
Every required fact has a reachable acquisition route.
contentModel.evidence: Strict simulation reached both endings without a missing required fact.

contentModel.item: Every scene uses valid stages, actors, anchors, props, flags, and facts.
contentModel.evidence: Strict validation passed all cross-reference checks.
This minimal one-shot package has no regeneration pipeline or human override layer.

playability.item: simulate-playthrough.mjs --strict reaches at least one ending.
Every reachable branch can reach an ending; no reachable route deadlocks or traps.
playability.evidence: Strict simulation explored all eight states and completed both routes.

playability.item: Every declared ending and required beat is reachable, or is explicitly marked optional.
playability.evidence: The simulator reached 8/8 beats and 2/2 endings.

playability.item: Blocking movement, audio, camera, dialogue, and interaction cues have fallbacks.
playability.evidence: Both blocking actor cues have positive 4000 ms timeouts and snap fallbacks; all other cues are nonblocking or bounded waits.

playability.item: A full playthrough succeeds without network access or runtime AI.
playability.evidence: The deterministic local simulator completed every route and assets is empty.

playability.item: Save/resume restores beat, flags, known facts, prop states, and player/NPC placement.
playability.evidence: The manifest declares the required save-state contract, but no host runtime was in scope to exercise persistence.

interactionAndUi.item: Context focus chooses the object the player reasonably intends.
interactionAndUi.evidence: Focus selection belongs to the host renderer; this package provides semantic interaction anchors only.

interactionAndUi.item: Every interaction displays a concrete verb and feedback.
interactionAndUi.evidence: All player actions use Continue or Inspect and apply visible state or progression effects.

interactionAndUi.item: The player can distinguish performance from exploration and can find the continue action.
interactionAndUi.evidence: Beat modes distinguish performance, conversation, exploration, and transition; actions explicitly label Continue or Inspect.

interactionAndUi.item: Dialogue, transcript, choices, journal, pause, errors, and ending screens fit target viewports.
interactionAndUi.evidence: No renderer or viewport is part of the requested data-only package.

interactionAndUi.item: Keyboard-only play and promised accessibility settings work.
interactionAndUi.evidence: Keyboard bindings and accessibility preferences are declared, but runtime input behavior is outside this package.

stagingAndPresentation.item: Actors do not overlap important props, leave the walkable area, or block all routes.
stagingAndPresentation.evidence: Coordinates and navigation belong to the host adapter by schema design.

stagingAndPresentation.item: Entrances, exits, gaze, lighting, sound, and prop state changes match cue intent.
stagingAndPresentation.evidence: Semantic cues reproduce Elias's entrance, the timetable flip, suitcase reveal, door unlock, Mara's exit, and lamp-lit ending.

stagingAndPresentation.item: Required visual and audio clues remain perceptible with accessibility alternatives.
stagingAndPresentation.evidence: Every required clue also becomes a text fact; progression does not depend solely on light, sound, color, or timing.

stagingAndPresentation.item: Placeholder art is coherent, labeled in the asset report, and free of license ambiguity.
stagingAndPresentation.evidence: reports/asset-license-report.json records generated geometric placeholder semantics and no third-party media.

stagingAndPresentation.item: Representative screenshots show no clipping, unreadable text, or unintended empty stage.
stagingAndPresentation.evidence: The requested deliverable has no renderer, so no screenshots can be produced.

engineeringAndDelivery.item: Runtime code is story-agnostic; switching a supported production does not require core runtime edits.
engineeringAndDelivery.evidence: No runtime code is included; production.json targets the reusable interactive-stage-production/v1 contract.

engineeringAndDelivery.item: Client code contains no secret keys and renders source/model text safely.
engineeringAndDelivery.evidence: No client code, network dependency, secret, or executable source markup is present.

engineeringAndDelivery.item: Errors name the scene, beat, cue, and fallback outcome.
engineeringAndDelivery.evidence: Runtime logging behavior is outside the data-only package.

engineeringAndDelivery.item: Package hashes are reproducible for unchanged input.
engineeringAndDelivery.evidence: The packaged source hash exactly matches the source inspection, and two consecutive pack runs produced identical content and archive SHA-256 values.

engineeringAndDelivery.item: Delivery includes source inspection, adaptation ledger, unresolved claims, validation summary, simulation summary, asset/license report, and run instructions supplied by the host project.
engineeringAndDelivery.evidence: All named reports and README.md are included.
